scope: Aligns supplied 2026 gravity/cosmology/dark-sector sources to internal frontier objects without promoting any theorem, empirical validation, or physical-discovery claim.

sources.title: Gravity2026: New Frontiers in Cosmology, Daejeon, South Korea
sources.alignment: Gravity-frontier workshop context: gravitational waves, early- and late-time cosmology, and modified gravity.

sources.title: An open system approach to gravity
sources.alignment: Open-system gravity framework relevant to controlled gravity-environment interface modeling.

sources.title: Gravity follows Newton and Einstein’s rules, even at cosmic scales
sources.alignment: Supports boundary discipline against claiming modified-gravity failure or replacement from the internal project.

sources.title: New research challenges the cold dark matter assumption
sources.alignment: Dark-sector model-space expansion: dark matter may originate hot and cool before structure formation.

sources.title: Dark matter may come in multiple forms, new model suggests
sources.alignment: Dark-sector plurality signal: supports model-space mapping, not dark-matter resolution.

sources.title: Discovery prospects for a minimal dark matter model at cosmic and intensity frontier experiments
sources.alignment: Maps dark-sector candidates to possible direct-detection, beam-dump, and intensity-frontier tests.

sources.title: Dark Matter and the Early Formation of Supermassive Black Holes
sources.alignment: Connects dark matter capture, black-hole growth, mergers, and early SMBH formation as a source-map input only.
